formula_note: Central value taken from Ungváry's rounded headline figure; widened to 35k-40k because the preface uses approximate language ('similar number').
uncertainty_note: No side-specific archival death audit was recovered in this quick-access pass; this is a rounded battle-scale estimate.
quality_note: Good battle headline, moderate rounding uncertainty.

formula_note: Central value from Ungváry's 'some 80,000 dead'; widened to 75k-85k to reflect explicit approximation.
uncertainty_note: Source does not cleanly separate Soviet from Romanian irrecoverable losses; combined attacker series retained at coalition level.
quality_note: Strong headline scale, but still rounded and coalition-level.

formula_note: Sum of side-specific military death estimates: Axis defenders plus Soviet-Romanian besiegers.
uncertainty_note: Military deaths are far better constrained than civilian direct deaths, but still rounded and not based here on a full archival fatality audit.
quality_note: Usable for core DR with moderate rounding uncertainty.

formula_note: Direct military-action civilian deaths explicitly stated by source.
uncertainty_note: Inflicting side is not separately identified in the source and should not be inferred.

inflicting_side: Arrow Cross Party / Hungarian fascist militias
formula_note: Bounded by a broader-window 10k-15k range and the siege-specific 15k statement; best value set conservatively below the siege-specific ceiling because one source's wider date window begins before encirclement.
uncertainty_note: Direct-execution status is clear, but exact episode matching is imperfect across available quick-access sources.
quality_note: Important side-attributed subseries, but the exact siege-window count is not perfectly locked down.

formula_note: Core direct-civilian estimate = 13,000 military-action deaths + 10,000-15,000 Arrow Cross execution deaths. Best = 13,000 + 12,000.
uncertainty_note: A stricter but less complete interpretation would count only the explicit 13,000 'military action' deaths, producing a substantially higher DR. This bundle instead includes source-grounded Arrow Cross executions as direct civilian deaths.
quality_note: Usable but driven by a mixed-source decomposition problem.

formula_note: No separate unknown-status direct-death series found in the quick-access source set.
uncertainty_note: Unknown-status victims are excluded under a strict rule rather than silently allocated to civilians or military.
quality_note: Null by design; prevents silent reclassification.

formula_note: Duration = 50 days = 1.64 months. Average exposed military strength assumed at 210k / 235k / 256k. Best scenario uses ~55k average defenders plus ~180k average besiegers. High scenario treats the combined 256k starting strength as an upper-bound average.
uncertainty_note: This is the most assumption-sensitive denominator in the Budapest bundle because daily force levels and rotation data were not recovered in the quick-access pass.
quality_note: Derived and assumption-sensitive, but still city-specific and clearly bounded.

formula_note: Duration = 50 days = 1.64 months. Based on 800k trapped civilians and no evacuation, average exposed civilian population is bounded at 740k / 780k / 800k across the episode.
uncertainty_note: Civilian denominator is better constrained than the military denominator because Budapest civilians were trapped in the city and not evacuated at scale.
quality_note: Good city-specific denominator with moderate attrition-adjustment uncertainty.

formula_note: DR_v1_direct = (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months). Low scenario uses mil low, mil-PM high, civ high, civ-PM low; best uses all best values; high uses mil high, mil-PM low, civ low, civ-PM high.
uncertainty_note: Budapest's DR is sensitive mainly to whether Arrow Cross executions are included in core direct civilian deaths and to average exposed military strength. Under a stricter 13k-only civilian direct count, DR would be substantially higher.
quality_note: Useful and clearly bounded, but driven by a non-trivial civilian numerator classification choice.
